name: Marduk
shortDescription: Marduk is the patron god of Babylon and the chief god of the Babylonian pantheon. He is associated with justice, compassion, healing, regeneration, magic, and fairness.
description: Marduk rose to prominence as the chief god of Babylon, eventually eclipsing earlier deities in importance. His origins are somewhat obscure, but he is generally considered to have been a storm god, associated with thunder, lightning, and rain. Marduk's most famous myth is his victory over Tiamat, the primordial goddess of the sea, in the Enuma Elish, the Babylonian creation epic. In this epic, Marduk is chosen by the other gods to confront Tiamat, who is threatening to destroy the cosmos. He defeats her in battle, splitting her body in two to create the heavens and the earth. He also establishes the order of the cosmos and assigns roles to the other gods. Marduk's temple in Babylon, the Esagila, was a major religious center, and his cult spread throughout Mesopotamia. He was seen as a benevolent and just ruler, and his worship was closely tied to the prosperity and stability of Babylon. He was also associated with divination and magic, and his priests were skilled in interpreting omens. Marduk's influence persisted for centuries, even after the decline of Babylon, and he remained a significant figure in Mesopotamian religion.

sources.title: Ancient Near Eastern Texts Relating to the Old Testament
sources.author: James B. Pritchard

sources.title: Gods, Demons and Symbols of Ancient Mesopotamia
sources.author: Jeremy Black and Anthony Green
